response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer with strong web and full-stack credentials, but his profile does not match this role's core requirements. His C++ experience is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project, versus the 8–12 years of hands-on C++/STL work the role demands. There is no evidence of industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL practices, MQTT, or message-broker-based large-scale C++ systems. His low-latency DLang work at Dunnhumby shows systems-programming aptitude, but the gap on the primary stack and security/industrial domain is too large for a senior C++ position.
strengths: Solid systems-programming exposure: low-latency, async I/O microservices handling millions of requests/sec (DLang)
Long, consistent career with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, testing tools, mentoring/workshops)
Event-driven and distributed-systems experience via GISCollective and microservices work
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~6 months, over a decade ago) against a C++-centric role
No industrial automation, IoT/MQTT, or IEC 62443 / SDL security background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js), not embedded/industrial platform engineering

key_factors.factor: Low-latency systems and event-driven architecture experience
